commit e82840d6a844d933ee3764ad37d87b6379299a0f
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jun 14 10:20:59 2026 +0200

    feat(update): add install script and self-update
    
    install.sh detects the host arch, downloads the matching release
    binary, verifies its SHA256, and installs to ~/.local/bin (no sudo).
    `decant update` fetches the latest release via ureq, verifies the
    checksum with sha2, and atomically replaces the running binary. A
    build.rs bakes the target triple so the binary knows which asset to
    fetch.

commit 120a5bce6b8ff05973028614d9a4aa24200c924a
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jun 14 10:20:59 2026 +0200

    chore(release): add release tooling and changelog
    
    cargo-release + git-cliff recipes (just release/changelog), a
    tag-triggered GitHub Actions workflow that cross-builds the four target
    binaries and publishes them with SHA256 checksums and git-cliff notes,
    a generated CHANGELOG.md (un-ignored from the blanket **/*.md rule),
    a pinned cliff.toml remote, and a release runbook in the README.

commit b80592688bb72da9c7642ed8eb2b231bc1ccb0eb
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jun 14 10:20:59 2026 +0200

    refactor(errors): migrate library crates to thiserror
    
    decant-agents and decant-store were the last library crates using
    anyhow; they now expose typed errors (AgentError, StoreError) like
    decant-core and decant-transforms already do. The binary keeps anyhow
    and converts the typed errors via `?`.

commit d1e6b8d3bd94adac8ef95af66bc11be19e0a7b53
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jun 14 09:17:45 2026 +0200

    feat(cli): add init, hook, and history subcommands
    
    `decant init [--project]` installs the agent hook (idempotent);
    `decant hook <agent>` is the runtime processor (stdout-only,
    passthrough on any error, no auto-approve); `decant history` reports
    actual reduction per command plus recurring no-config commands. `decant
    run` now records each run to the metrics store best-effort. Adds
    black-box CLI tests for hook, init, and history.

commit 6367902614828acb4386daf864b4dfe19b7644e3
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jun 14 09:17:45 2026 +0200

    feat(store): add SQLite metrics store crate
    
    decant-store persists one row per `decant run` (command, bytes/tokens
    in and out, duration, config source) and aggregates them for the
    history report. DB path resolves to $DECANT_DB_PATH, else
    $XDG_DATA_HOME/decant, else ~/.local/share/decant. Unit-tested
    in-memory.

commit 2996c9e86b6a5b86074b701226b586578931eb57
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jun 14 09:17:45 2026 +0200

    feat(agents): add agent hook integration crate
    
    A multi-agent hook seam for routing an agent's commands through
    `decant run`. The Agent trait (one impl per file) owns per-agent hook
    concerns; ClaudeAgent is the first impl. An agent-agnostic shell-aware
    rewriter wraps simple commands (cargo test -> decant run -- cargo test)
    while passing builtins, pipelines, and redirects through unchanged.
    settings.json merging is idempotent.
    
    Carries this batch's workspace deps: serde_json, tempfile, and rusqlite
    (used by the metrics store in the next commit).

commit e3cbf3745bcfd0e77560d980bcb218de19405145
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jun 13 18:30:53 2026 +0200

    chore: adopt the platform .gitignore
    
    Replace the minimal ignore list with the squadri platform's fuller
    set: credential/secret patterns, IDE and macOS junk, env files, and
    .claude/ allowlisting. Docs stay ignored via a blanket `**/*.md`
    rule (README/TODO/CLAUDE excepted), which also silently ignores any
    new non-README markdown by default.

commit bcd4d77252ba318403994aced936b9631827f8ee
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jun 13 18:29:38 2026 +0200

    chore: vendor decant-relevant Claude skills
    
    Keep rust-documenter, rust-quality, commit, and find-docs as
    project skills. Drop the platform/service-oriented skills
    (rust-tracing-instrument, validate-implementation,
    env-vars-documenter) — decant is a sync CLI with no tracing or
    service config — and the generic code-documenter, superseded by
    rust-documenter for a pure-Rust project.

commit f89deeee7b41c84e360331fe4c61f90ee49f051d
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jun 13 16:09:07 2026 +0200

    docs: document decant-core and decant-metrics
    
    Add crate- and module-level docs, # Errors sections on the
    Result-returning APIs, per-variant docs on RunError, and field
    docs across the foundation crates. Add runnable doc-tests for the
    metrics functions and enrich the crate READMEs.

commit 2888d13faa799187e03f8e8c43211b51a4195dce
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jun 13 16:08:56 2026 +0200

    feat(transforms): add TOML-driven chainable transform system
    
    Replace the hardcoded Identity transform with per-command routing.
    A 7-rule vocabulary (strip_ansi, drop, keep, keep_after, collapse,
    dedup, truncate) — each a Rule trait impl in its own file under
    rules/ — composes into a RuleChain via TOML configs. The router
    resolves the wrapped command's (program, subcommand) to a config
    (project -> user -> embedded built-in -> identity); built-ins are
    embedded with include_dir and auto-discovered. The CLI gains
    routing in `run`, a `--raw` bypass, and an `explain` subcommand.
    
    Ships built-ins for cargo build/test/nextest with snapshot and
    >=60% savings tests against real fixtures. A bad config (invalid
    TOML or regex) warns and falls back to identity, never blocking
    output.

commit 41d1a95320827196fd7a6cdf2b74a496da53899c
Author: DavidB <berbsd@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jun 13 14:08:37 2026 +0200

    feat: scaffold decant workspace with v1 capture pipeline
    
    decant wraps a child command, captures its output, transforms it to
    reduce LLM token usage, and emits the result. This is the v1 foundation:
    plumbing + trait seams, with an Identity (passthrough) transform.
    
    Workspace (edition 2024, resolver 3, toolchain 1.95, conventions from
    squadri/platform — shared lints, deny/lefthook/taplo/typos/nextest/just/CI):
    
    - decant-core: the engine. Runner + Transform trait seams, the execute()
      pipeline (transform on clean exit, raw passthrough on timeout), and
      CaptureRunner — a threaded-drain runner with idle + wall-clock timeouts,
      process-group kill (SIGTERM -> grace -> SIGKILL), 128+signal exit codes,
      and exit 124 on timeout.
    - decant-transforms: Identity passthrough (Buffered mode).
    - decant-metrics: Measurement / savings_pct / token estimation.
    - tools/decant: lib + thin bin CLI. `decant run -- <cmd>` emits via
      std::io::Write (raw bytes), stats/markers to stderr, exit code propagated.
      Logic lives in the library (run_cli/dispatch/run) for testability.
    
    23 tests pass (unit + black-box CLI integration); clippy --all-targets clean;
    cargo deny clean. Design docs are kept local-only (gitignored).
